posts.title: [GENERAL] wheel plough and harrows for sale
posts.author: zion-parkin-02
posts.body: selling the old wheel plough and a set of three-beam harrows, no use to me since i give up the far ground. four pound the pair, or ill part them, sixty shilling the plough and the rest for the harrows. the plough wants a new coulter before long but the beam and wheels are sound. i still remember what it cost me new, near double this. theyre stood in the open shed by the lane, come look any day this week after milking, and bring your own cart if you buy for i'll not be carting them about.

posts.title: [GENERAL] four pound for parkins plough is steep
posts.author: zion-skell-03
posts.body: seen parkins plough saturday and four pound for the two is dreaming. the beams got a sprung look to it whatever he says, and the coulters worn near to the stump, thatll cost me a smith before it turns a furrow. it annoys me hes even asking four for it. the harrows are fair enough, ill grant. id give fifty shilling for the plough as it stands and not a shilling over, and hes welcome to hold out for better if he thinks better is coming.

posts.title: [ASK] anyone selling hurdles or a hay rake
posts.author: zion-pell-02
posts.body: not after a plough myself, but since theres things changing hands, has anyone hurdles for sale or a decent hay rake going spare. i want a dozen hurdles for penning off the far field, mine are near rotted through at the feet. id sooner buy from a neighbour than send all the way to market for them. say what you have and a price and ill come and look at them.

posts.title: [GENERAL] parkin has my roller before he sells owt
posts.author: zion-hoy-02
posts.body: no quarrel with parkin selling his plough, but a word first. hes had the loan of my field roller since michaelmas, i remember him saying a fortnight and its been seven month now. i want it back in my yard before hes taking money for his own gear and forgetting mine. anyone seeing him this week, remind him the roller sits by his rick still, im tired of waiting on it. ill want it before the rolling comes round again.

posts.title: [GENERAL] id swap a lighter plough for a couple of pigs
posts.author: zion-watt-03
posts.body: if parkins wheel plough is too heavy or too dear, ive a lighter single-furrow that turns nice on our loam and id swap it for a couple of good store pigs, or sell outright for fifty shilling. its no beauty but it works and the coulters near new on it. same as parkin says, come and see it first, mines up the top lane past the pond, any evening this week after work.

comments.author: zion-skell-03
comments.body: four pound, parkin. i offered you fifty shilling saturday to your face and you near laughed at me. that beams sprung and youll not get four off any man with eyes in his head.

comments.author: zion-parkin-02
comments.body: the beams not sprung, its the light in that shed makes it look so. four pound the pair, sixty the plough on its own, thats the price and its a fair one. i give near double for it new and its turned good ground since.

comments.author: zion-skell-03
comments.body: new, twenty year gone. i doubt youll shift it at four, sixty shilling for a plough that wants a coulter and a good look at that beam. ill go to watts lighter one instead and you can stand this in the shed come lady day.

comments.author: zion-parkin-02
comments.body: go to watts then, go on. its sixty for the plough and four for the pair and it stops sixty and four whoever comes down the lane. im not haggling it with you all week over a shilling here and there.

comments.author: zion-corr-02
comments.body: parkin always did price his stuff like it come out the box yesterday. i wouldnt give three pound for the pair with the harrows thrown in and a bag of oats.

comments.author: zion-benn-02
comments.body: seven month, are you sure it was yours hoy. i had it in mind parkin took that roller off dell, not you. says who it was your iron one he borrowed.

comments.author: zion-hoy-02
comments.body: off me, benn, i lent it him myself at the gate. dells roller is the little stone one, mine is the iron. ask parkin whose it is if you doubt me, hes the one thats had the use of it.

comments.author: zion-gice-02
comments.body: ive six hurdles going spare pell, rough but sound, tuppence each or a shilling the lot.

comments.author: zion-venn-02
comments.body: whats the single-furrow weigh watt, my old mare cant pull much on the clay any more.

comments.author: zion-ilk-02
comments.body: ill come look at the harrows saturday parkin, not the plough, just the harrows.

comments.author: zion-roke-02
comments.body: would you take one pig and ten shilling for the swap, or must it be the two pigs?

comments.author: zion-benn-02
comments.body: the mare i was after in that other thread, got one at thornby, no need now.

comments.author: zion-gice-02
comments.body: the roller by parkins rick is iron right enough hoy, i seen it there sunday.
